title: primula × pubescens
intro: Primula × pubescens, also known as the marsh primrose or marsh celandine, is a hybrid plant species within the Primula genus, resulting from the cross between Primula palustris and Primula elatior. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Primula × pubescens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe respiratory issues. Primula × pubescens can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic use.
